what can contour plowing reduce what can contour plowing reduce what can contour plowing reduce what can contour plowing reduce what can contour plowing reduce Contour plowing is an agricultural technique that significantly reduces soil erosion. By plowing and planting along the natural contours of the land rather than up and down slopes, this method helps to create a series of ridges that act as small dams. These dams slow the flow of water, allowing more time for it to soak into the soil rather than washing away topsoil and seeds. Consequently, contour plowing not only conserves soil but also enhances water retention, which can improve crop yields and reduce the need for irrigation. Additionally, this practice can reduce nutrient loss, as water runoff is minimized, thereby maintaining the fertility of the soil.
